description: This figure presents a scatter plot showing the dependence of the austenite lattice parameter on silicon and aluminum alloying content in an austenitic steel system. The x-axis displays relative alloying additions in weight percent (0 to ~3 wt pct), while the y-axis shows the austenite lattice parameter in nanometers (0.358 to 0.360 nm). Three distinct data points are plotted: the base alloy (0 wt pct addition) with a lattice parameter of approximately 0.3585 nm; the 2.5 wt pct Al alloy, which exhibits a markedly increased lattice parameter of approximately 0.3598 nm; and the 2.5 wt pct Si alloy, showing a minimal increase to approximately 0.3587 nm. The visual comparison demonstrates that aluminum alloying significantly expands the austenite lattice, whereas silicon has a comparatively negligible effect on the lattice parameter. These measured lattice parameter values, obtained via X-ray diffraction, provide the critical structural input for subsequent stacking fault energy calculations and support the conclusion that Al and Si additions produce qualitatively different effects on the austenite crystal structure despite both being added at 2.5 wt pct levels.
